topic.what: Impact of anonymization placement (dataset vs answer) in RAG pipelines on utility-privacy trade-offs
topic.how: Systematically tested 6 anonymization methods (3 PII-based, 3 DP-based) at PRE and POST stages across 3 datasets, measuring ROUGE-L, cosine similarity, perplexity, and LLM-judge privacy scores
topic.why_matters: No prior work examined where anonymization should occur in RAG pipelines; placement significantly affects both utility and privacy, informing best practices for privacy risk mitigation

characteristics.what_it_measures: ROUGE-L between baseline and anonymized summaries
characteristics.context: POST anonymization consistently yields higher utility than PRE across non-DP methods; DP-based methods degrade utility significantly across both placements

characteristics.what_it_measures: Cosine similarity of sentence embeddings between baseline and anonymized summaries
characteristics.context: POST consistently outperforms PRE for non-DP methods; DP methods show mixed results

characteristics.what_it_measures: Perplexity of generated summaries via GPT-2
characteristics.context: DP-MLM produces extremely high perplexity (poor coherence) especially POST; non-DP methods maintain reasonable coherence
PRE anonymization achieves lower leakage; PII deletion is best for BBC (8 PRE, 23 POST); DP methods trade utility for privacy
Non-DP methods applied POST yield best trade-offs; simple PII deletion outperforms complex DP methods on trade-off metric
Lower ε = stronger privacy but worse utility; trade-off confirmed across all DP methods

characteristics.what_it_measures: Summary quality via ROUGE-L across summarization task
characteristics.value: Best single result: PII Deletion POST BBC RL=0.93
characteristics.context: POST anonymization with non-DP methods achieves near-baseline utility while providing privacy protection
Three diverse datasets covering news, emails, and legal documents; 20,800 total generated answers evaluated

good_for: Evaluating anonymization placement strategies in RAG systems
Comparing PII-based vs differential privacy anonymization methods
Building privacy-preserving RAG applications with utility guarantees
Reproducing experiments via open-source GuardRAG tool
not_for: Real-time production RAG systems requiring minimal latency
Scenarios requiring formal differential privacy guarantees with minimal epsilon
Applications where utility loss is unacceptable (use PRE anonymization only)
Large-scale document collections beyond the tested 800 documents
requires: LlamaIndex and Pinecone for RAG implementation

novelty: First systematic comparison of anonymization placement (PRE vs POST) in RAG pipelines
First analysis showing that mitigation placement significantly affects privacy-utility trade-offs
Introduction of composite trade-off metric TO for evaluating anonymization effectiveness
Open-source GuardRAG tool for reproducible experimentation
